evidence: requireNextArg in Main.java has two consecutive Javadoc blocks — a short draft ('Returns args[i], exiting with a helpful message...') immediately followed by a longer replacement ('Returns the next argument from args at index i...') — classic AI-generated draft artifact

Several Javadoc comments restate the obvious: QueryResults private constructor 'Utility class - prevent instantiation' on a record-like class; LogDiceCalculator class doc 'Utility class for computing association scores' followed by a 'Supported measures' list that the method signatures already communicate
WordSketchResult.UNKNOWN_POS constant has a Javadoc 'Sentinel for missing POS information returned by the tagger' — the field name already communicates this; the comment adds no new information
handleFullDependencySketch Javadoc: 'Handle full dependency sketch request. Returns all dependency relations for the given lemma.' — this restates the method name verbatim
No noisy debug entry/exit logging detected; logger.debug calls are selective and purposeful
No null-check sprawl on typed non-nullable values detected beyond the nullable parameters that are genuinely optional

strengths: Debug logging is selective and targeted — no entry/exit logging on every method
No generic names (handleData, processItem) in domain code — domain vocabulary is used consistently
No null-check sprawl on typed values that are known non-null at their call sites
No pass-through wrapper functions with no added logic detected
ai_generated_debt.issue_character: AI-generated debt is limited to Javadoc redundancy: one double-draft comment block and a handful of Javadoc comments that restate the code without adding insight, rather than systemic boilerplate or defensive overengineering.
ai_generated_debt.score_rationale: The codebase shows only modest AI-generated debt — no defensive sprawl, no entry/exit logging, no generic domain names. The score does not reach 90+ because the double-Javadoc on requireNextArg is a clear unresolved draft artifact, and several Javadoc blocks across the codebase restate method names without adding insight. These are cosmetic but real signals of AI-assisted drafting that was not fully edited.

issues.summary: requireNextArg has two consecutive Javadoc comment blocks — an unresolved draft immediately preceding the final version
evidence: First block: '/** Returns {@code args[i]}, exiting with a helpful message if {@code i} is out of bounds. */'

Second block immediately follows: '/** Returns the next argument from {@code args} at index {@code i}...

Caution...*/'
The first block is a draft that was superseded and should have been deleted; Javadoc tooling silently discards it, leaving noise
issues.suggestion: Delete the first shorter Javadoc block. Retain only the complete second block with the Caution note about System.exit.

issues.summary: Several Javadoc blocks restate the method name or field name without adding domain insight
evidence: handleFullDependencySketch Javadoc: 'Handle full dependency sketch request. Returns all dependency relations for the given lemma.' — identical to what the method name already communicates
QueryResults private constructor Javadoc: 'Utility class - prevent instantiation' — standard boilerplate that adds nothing beyond the private modifier
WordSketchResult.UNKNOWN_POS field Javadoc restates 'Sentinel for missing POS information returned by the tagger' — the constant name UNKNOWN_POS already communicates this fully
issues.suggestion: Remove or replace Javadoc on handleFullDependencySketch with a comment explaining the dispatch rationale (why it delegates to handleDepSketchForType). Remove the QueryResults constructor comment. The UNKNOWN_POS comment could note the contract: callers should check for this value before displaying POS information.

root_causes: Double-Javadoc on requireNextArg is an unresolved AI-assisted iterative drafting artifact where the second version was written without deleting the first
Restating Javadoc is a systemic pattern from AI-generated comment stubs that were not curated after generation
